title: ACT Manifest auth.schemes — security-relevant shape (PRD-109)
description: Per PRD-109-R6, PRD-109-R7, PRD-109-R8. PRD-109 fixes the security-relevant shape of the manifest's auth.schemes array. The full manifest envelope and all non-security-relevant fields are owned by PRD-100/PRD-106; this schema is the security-constrained subset that PRD-109 normatively imposes. The array is ordered by server preference, most-preferred first (PRD-109-R6, gap C3). The `kind` enum is closed at v0.1; adding a value is MAJOR per PRD-108-R5(4). Conditional sub-schema enforces OAuth2 required fields per PRD-109-R7.

schemes.description: Ordered list of supported auth schemes. Order MUST reflect server preference, most-preferred first. Consumers SHOULD attempt schemes in advertised order.

kind.description: Closed enum at v0.1. Adding a value is a MAJOR change per PRD-108.

header_name.description: For api_key: the HTTP header name to send the key in. SHOULD default to 'Authorization' with a 'Bearer <key>' value to avoid header proliferation.

scopes_supported.description: Required when kind == 'oauth2'. MUST be non-empty. The minimum scope 'act.read' is reserved by PRD-109; OAuth schemes MUST advertise at least 'act.read' or a superset.
